beats.activity: Wake up, pour-over coffee ritual, light breakfast at home
beats.reasoning: Logan's morning ritual is sacred—he always starts with a quiet pour-over. Low energy from yesterday (40/100) means he'll linger at home before heading out.

beats.activity: Drive to work; transit strike means no bus option, rideshare surge pricing makes personal car the better choice
beats.reasoning: Metro buses and Link suspended until 4pm due to citywide strike. Driving from Northgate to Interbay is ~7 miles; budget 45 min to account for strike-related traffic congestion and avoiding surge-priced rideshare.

beats.activity: Morning shift as barista and shift lead; manage team through steady morning and mid-morning rush
beats.reasoning: Typical morning shift for a barista in a busy shop. Partly cloudy weather means moderate foot traffic. As shift lead, Logan will be training and managing the team.

beats.activity: Lunch break: grab a sandwich and sit quietly nearby, camera in hand
beats.reasoning: Logan prefers quiet lunch breaks to recharge. He'll stay local in Interbay rather than drive across town given the transit strike and surge pricing.

beats.activity: Afternoon shift as barista and shift lead; slower mid-afternoon allows time to mentor newer staff
beats.reasoning: Logan works through the afternoon shift, wrapping up as transit strike ends at 4pm. He stays engaged with his team and the rhythm of the shop.

beats.activity: Drive home; transit is now restored after 4pm, but personal car still preferred after strike day
beats.reasoning: Post-strike commute should be manageable now. Budget 45 min for Interbay to Northgate drive, accounting for lingering congestion from strike.

beats.activity: Rest and freshen up at home; change clothes, grab camera
beats.reasoning: Logan needs downtime after managing a full shift during transit chaos. He'll prep his camera for an evening shoot, energized by the partly cloudy conditions and golden hour light.

beats.activity: Evening birdwatching and photography at Green Lake; catching herons and other waterfowl in the golden hour
beats.reasoning: Perfect evening light for photography, and Logan loves capturing herons at dusk. Green Lake is his go-to meditative spot. Drive from Northgate is ~15 min.
Short drive back to Northgate, ~15 min. Logan will be winding down after an evening in nature.

beats.activity: Home leisure: sort through contact sheets, listen to lo-fi, prepare camera for next shoot
beats.reasoning: Logan's typical wind-down routine. Low energy carries over from yesterday, so he'll keep it simple and introspective at home before bed.
Full night's rest after a strike-day shift. Sleep span is 7h 45m, ending well before 06:00 of next day as required.

world_event_prompt: Mild, partly cloudy Thursday, high of 68F. Citywide transit strike: all Metro buses and Link Light Rail suspended until 4pm. Rideshare surge pricing in effect.
